channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Four arrested in Vancouver following alleged $200,000 casino cheating investigation
item.description: Four individuals—two men and two women—were arrested in Vancouver following an investigation into an alleged casino cheating operation involving more than $200,000. According to authorities, suspected...

item.title: Black bear euthanized in Golden Ears Park after repeated human conflict incidents
item.description: A female black bear was euthanized by the B.C. Conservation Officer Service in Golden Ears Provincial Park near Maple Ridge after exhibiting increasingly aggressive behaviour and repeated conflicts wi...

item.title: Eleven-year-old boy rescued from burning storage container; 13-year-old arrested
item.description: An 11-year-old boy was rescued from a burning storage container at Trimsaran Rugby Club in Carmarthenshire, South Wales, thanks to the quick actions of several local fathers. The group, who were enjoy...

item.title: Jury deliberates in Alberta highway shooting murder trial involving two accused men
item.description: Jurors have begun deliberations in the second-degree murder trial of two men accused of fatally shooting a stranger on a southern Alberta highway after a chaotic roadside incident involving a burning ...

item.title: Why the Philippines is pushing for algorithmic and platform accountability in social media regulation
item.description: The article discusses the growing legislative effort in the Philippines to address disinformation by focusing not only on individual users, but also on the algorithms and platforms that shape online c...

item.title: India-Russia discuss Arctic trade corridor and defence cooperation
item.description: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval held talks with Nikolai Patrushev, the Russian President’s aide and chairman of Russia’s Maritime Board, in Moscow on May 29. The discussions reviewed progress on ...

item.title: White House Considers Ending Trump’s Controversial Legal Fund Amid GOP Concerns
item.description: White House officials are reportedly evaluating whether to discontinue President Donald Trump's $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund,' a legal settlement widely criticized as a slush fund for allie...

item.title: UN Warns Israeli Expansion in Gaza Will Increase Suffering for Children
item.description: The United Nations has raised serious concerns over Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's directive to take control of 70 percent of Gaza territory. This move comes despite a fragile ceasefire t...

item.title: Yeng Guiao stays optimistic as Rain or Shine falls to Ginebra in Game 5
item.description: Rain or Shine head coach Yeng Guiao remained composed and optimistic after his team suffered a Game 5 defeat against Barangay Ginebra in their PBA playoff series. Despite the setback, Guiao emphasized...

item.title: Evaluating Cash Payments Versus Formal Childcare in Australia
item.description: The debate around childcare in Australia has intensified as families face rising costs and limited improvements in outcomes for children. Over the last decade, both Coalition and Labor governments hav...

item.title: EIU Reports Dangote Refinery Reducing Nigeria’s Reliance on Fuel Imports and Boosting Forex Earnings
item.description: According to a report by the Economist Intelligence Unit (EIU), the Dangote Petroleum Refinery is significantly changing Nigeria’s downstream oil sector by reducing the country’s long-standing depende...

item.title: Greek national charged over alleged surveillance of London-based Iranian journalist using covert camera
item.description: A Greek national has appeared in Westminster Magistrates’ Court accused of assisting a foreign intelligence service in an alleged surveillance operation targeting a London-based Iranian journalist. Io...

item.title: Himachal Pradesh to experience rain and thunderstorms till June 4, MeT issues advisory
item.description: The Meteorological Department has forecast a wet spell across Himachal Pradesh till June 4, 2026, issuing alerts for thunderstorms, lightning, and strong winds reaching 40-50 kmph. The advisory covers...

item.title: Sikho Nqothole beats Charlie Edwards on points in IBF junior-bantamweight eliminator
item.description: South African boxer Sikho Nqothole produced a composed and clinical performance in London on Friday night, outpointing former world champion Charlie Edwards in an IBF junior-bantamweight title elimina...

item.title: Fifty men’s summer style tips under £100 from a menswear expert
item.description: This article offers a comprehensive menswear guide for summer 2026, focusing on practical, affordable style updates mostly costing under £100. It frames summer dressing as a challenge of balancing com...

item.title: Meaning of 'kerida' featured as Word of the Day for May 30, 2026
item.description: The article from ABS-CBN Lifestyle introduces the Word of the Day for May 30, 2026, which is the term “kerida.” As part of its regular language and lifestyle feature, the publication highlights words ...

item.title: Líl̓wat First Nation challenges B.C. decision to reduce Joffre Lakes park closure days
item.description: The Líl̓wat First Nation is raising concerns about a decision by the British Columbia government to significantly reduce planned closure dates at Joffre Lakes Park, a popular recreational destination....

item.description: Kiki Rice has rapidly emerged as an important young player for Toronto’s WNBA expansion team, the Tempo, following a whirlwind stretch that began with a national championship at UCLA. After helping th...

item.title: KZN Police Prepare for Trucking Protest and Possible Road Blockages
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal police have raised alarms over a planned trucking protest organised by the All Truck Drivers Forum South Africa (ATDFSA), scheduled for 30 May 2026. Authorities are urging organisers to ...
